name: AIM-120C
_notes: March-only motor. Thrust = Isp(234s)*(51.26kg/6.5s)*9.807=18097N. ccm_k0 not in weapons_table entry; estimated 0.3 for modern ARH (community consensus).

name: AIM-7MH
_notes: Dual-stage: boost 25191N/3.7s, march 4141N/10.8s, avg 9516N. SARH chaff vulnerability 0.55 (chaff can break CW illuminator lock; higher than ARH).

name: AIM-9X
_source: pydcs 84.46kg; AIM-9JULI.lua proxy (M=82.3, ccm_k0=0.75, Nr_max=32). AIM-9X Block II imaging seeker ccm_k0 estimated 0.1 (excellent IRCCM).
_notes: AIM-9JULI ccm_k0=0.75 (confirmed datamine). AIM-9X Block II imaging seeker dramatically better vs flares; community and real-world sources estimate near-immunity. Using 0.1.

name: R-77
_source: P_77.lua (rockets/ datamine): ccm_k0=0.2 confirmed. mass=175kg, Mach_max=4, Range_max=50000m, PN_gain=4.
_notes: ccm_k0=0.2 confirmed from P_77 datamine file. R-77 is relatively resistant to chaff due to modern ARH seeker. Grid fins add drag; referenceArea effective ~0.02m2.

name: R-27ER
_notes: Boost 54780N/2.5s + march 34295N/5.5s, avg 42741N. SARH: chaff vulnerability 0.55 (same rationale as AIM-7MH). Primary defense is notching (doppler blind zone).

name: R-73
_source: P_73 present in datamine rockets/. AIM-9JULI ccm_k0=0.75 (same generation IR seeker). R-73 ccm_k0=0.75 estimated.
_notes: R-73 uses similar IR seeker generation to AIM-9M. DCS AIM-9JULI ccm_k0=0.75 used as direct analogue. mass=105kg, Nr_max=40G.

name: MICA EM
_source: MICA_R.lua: gload_limit=24G confirmed. 112kg, Mach 4. ccm_k0 estimated 0.2 (modern ARH, similar to R-77).
_notes: DCS MICA_R.lua: gload_limit=24G. ccm_k0 not confirmed in public file; estimated 0.2 matching R-77 (modern generation ARH).

name: SD-10
_notes: Boost 22783N/6s + march 6712N/4s, avg 16338N. S=0.03235m2. ccm_k0 not in published file; estimated 0.25 (modern ARH, slightly worse than R-77 based on community reports).

name: AIM-9J
_source: pydcs / USAF historical data. AIM-9J introduced 1977 with DSQ-29 seeker (uncooled, rear-aspect only).
_notes: Uncooled IR seeker, rear-aspect only. Must be pre-boresighted before launch. Very susceptible to flares (ccm_k0=0.92). Low gLimit (22G) reflects older airframe. seekerAcquisitionRange 1.5nm reflects short acquisition envelope. mass/thrust from pydcs AIM-9J entry.

name: AIM-9M
_source: Quaggles/dcs-lua-datamine AIM-9JULI.lua (DCS 2.9.16) used as proxy; AIM-9M mass ~86kg (pydcs). ccm_k0 estimated 0.70 (improved IRCCM vs 9J/9L but not 9X-class).
_notes: DCS AIM-9JULI ccm_k0=0.75 confirmed. AIM-9M has improved IRCCM (background discrimination, improved reticle) over 9L/9J; estimated 0.70 based on community consensus. Cooled seeker, all-aspect capable but still susceptible to modern flares.

name: R.550 Magic 1
_source: Jane's All the World's Aircraft / Matra data. R.550 Magic 1 entered service 1975. Mass 89kg confirmed multiple sources.
_notes: First-generation French IR AAM. Uncooled seeker, rear-aspect only. Very high agility (35G) despite old seeker. Highly susceptible to flares (ccm_k0=0.88). 3nm effective range. DCS Magic 1 analogue parameters used as reference.

name: R.550 Magic 2
_source: MBDA / Dassault specifications. R.550 Magic 2 entered service 1986. Mass 89kg confirmed.
_notes: Improved second-generation French IR AAM. Cooled seeker, all-aspect capable, improved IRCCM vs Magic 1 (ccm_k0=0.55). Retains Magic 1 exceptional agility. 6nm effective range reflects all-aspect capability. DCS Magic 2 ccm_k0 not confirmed; 0.55 estimated (comparable to 9M generation).

name: R-27ET
_source: Quaggles/dcs-lua-datamine P_27TE.lua (DCS 2.9.16). Same dual-stage motor as R-27ER; IR seeker replaces SARH seeker.
_notes: IR variant of R-27ER. Motor identical: boost 54780N/2.5s + march 34295N/5.5s, avg 42741N. Mass slightly lighter than ER (~343kg vs 351kg, smaller seeker head). IR seeker limits acquisition range to ~7nm vs SARH unlimited illumination. ccm_k0=0.75 estimated (improved IR seeker generation but large body). Invisible to RWR unlike R-27ER.

name: R-60
_notes: Soviet close-range dogfight IR AAM, introduced 1973. Very small and light (44kg). Old uncooled seeker, extremely susceptible to flares (ccm_k0=0.90). 2nm effective range. 30G maneuverability. DCS P_60 mass=44kg confirmed.

name: R-60M
_source: Quaggles/dcs-lua-datamine P_60M.lua (DCS 2.9.16). Improved seeker with partial IRCCM.
_notes: Upgraded R-60 with improved IR seeker (cooling, better reticle). ccm_k0=0.75 estimated (similar generation to R-73 but shorter range airframe). Slightly heavier than base R-60 (45kg). Increased seekerAcquisitionRange to 2.5nm reflects improved sensitivity.
